draft.introduction: Yoga Sutra Book 3 (Vibhuti Pada) describes the supernatural powers (siddhis) that arise from advanced meditation, while warning that attachment to them is itself an obstacle to liberation. It maps the deepest stages of contemplative attainment.

translation.text: Concentration (dharana) is the binding of the mind to one place. Meditation (dhyana) is the continuous flow of cognition toward that object. Absorption (samadhi) is when that same meditation shines forth as the object alone, as if empty of its own form. These three together constitute samyama (perfect discipline).

translator_notes.note: The progression dharana-dhyana-samadhi describes increasing absorption where the distinction between meditator and object dissolves. This phenomenology of contemplative states finds close parallels in Buddhist jhana practice, Christian mystical prayer (Teresa of Avila’s stages), and Sufi fana.
